channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: Small plane crashes in France, killing two
item.description: A light aircraft crashed on Monday in Fontainebleau, France, killing the pilot and passenger.

item.title: Trump to visit China this week
item.description: Beijing confirmed on Monday that the trip will mark the first visit by a sitting US president to China since 2017.

item.title: Qatari tanker becomes first ship to cross Strait of Hormuz
item.description: LNG shipments through the Strait of Hormuz show signs of revival as Qatar resumes exports on a key route previously blocked by regional tensions.

item.title: Oil firm Aramco reports huge net profits as prices surge
item.description: Brent crude hits $60 per barrel after Gulf military escalation; Aramco profits climb to new highs despite operational hurdles.

item.title: Eurovision festivities tarnished by widespread boycott
item.description: The 70th Eurovision Song Contest opens amid controversy and calls for political boycott. Austria braces for pro-Palestinian and pro-Israeli demonstrations.

item.title: Hantavirus-affected ship reaches shores of Tenerife
item.description: The Hondius ship's outbreak of the lethal Andes hantavirus has triggered international medical evacuations in Spain, as fears mount over its deadly toll.

item.title: Hantavirus: Three more Belgians quarantined
item.description: Health teams monitor six exposed Belgians as cruise virus risks unfold — two remain abroad with repatriation imminent.

item.title: German airports warn about 'millions' of cancelled flights
item.description: Flights to smaller destinations face cuts amid a jet fuel shortage. German airports predict higher fares and a 10% drop in capacity.

item.title: President of European Council hails 'new chapter' in Hungary
item.description: A major shift in Hungary: EU flag returns to parliament as officials aim to rebuild ties with Europe after nationalist leadership ends.

item.title: Hantavirus: Ship to arrive in Spain early on Sunday
item.description: WHO joins Spain in handling a cruise ship's hantavirus outbreak. Passengers will disembark in the Canary Islands as officials confirm no further cases onboard.

item.title: German Chancellor Merz criticises Slovakian presence in Moscow
item.description: Amid focus on NATO’s strength, Slovak PM’s Moscow visit sparks division. German chancellor voices concern over alliance cohesion.

item.title: Elon Musk, under judicial investigation, insults magistrates
item.description: Musk dismisses French magistrates with an insult as investigators scrutinise X's role in child pornography cases. A February raid and ignored summons add to tensions.

item.title: Putin: 'Russia is facing aggressive forces backed by NATO'
item.description: In a fiery Moscow address, Putin insists Russia's mission in Ukraine mirrors past battles against Nazism.

item.title: WHO confirms sixth case of hantavirus
item.description: Three fatalities and six confirmed cases of hantavirus—WHO addresses outbreak on an Atlantic cruise ship travelling to Spain, highlighting human-transmissible risks linked to Andes virus.

item.title: Belgium to spend billions on US missiles
item.description: A €3 billion missile deal reveals Belgium's bold military plans and ambitions to host American defence production. But conflicting interests may complicate the path forward.

item.title: Belgian performance art on display at Venice Biennale
item.description: Belgium’s historic pavilion turned protest site as artists challenge Israel’s presence at the Venice Biennale.

item.title: Moscow and Kyiv agree to three-day ceasfire, POW swap
item.description: Russia and Ukraine agree to a three-day ceasefire and prisoner swap.

item.title: US court throws out Trump's 10% global import tariff
item.description: The US Court of International Trade rules against Trump's 10% global tariff on imports into the USA, deeming it unlawful.

item.title: Alarming rise in infant mortality due to famine in Gaza, says MSF
item.description: Child mortality was almost non-existent before the conflict began, but cases of infant malnutrition started emerging in January 2024.

item.title: New York judge publishes alleged Epstein suicide note
item.description: The handwritten letter, kept sealed for years, appears to convey the convicted sex criminal's frustration and resignation.
